channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Gene Shalit, longtime 'Today' show movie critic, dies at 100
item.description: Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Known for his puffy hair, oversized handlebar mustache and a love for puns, Gene Shalit joined Today in 1970 and became arts editor in 1973. He was a middle-of-the-road critic, known for his wit and intelligence.

(Image credit: Richard Drew)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's name is still on the Kennedy Center — officials say it will be down by noon
item.description: President Donald Trump's name remained on the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday despite a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump's name remained on the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday despite a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building.

(Image credit: Cliff Owen)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: To read more this summer, stop waiting for the perfect moment
item.description: So you want to ignite a reading habit this summer. How do you get back into the groove? We talk to reading enthusiasts for their best tricks — like allowing yourself to read wherever, whenever.
item.content:encoded: So you want to ignite a reading habit this summer. How do you get back into the groove? We talk to reading enthusiasts for their best tricks — like allowing yourself to read wherever, whenever.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Spielberg returns to familiar alien territory in 'Disclosure Day'
item.description: Spielberg's new thriller centers on a massive U.S. conspiracy to hide the fact that aliens have been visiting Earth for decades. If anything, though, the movie's pleasures feel more retro than timely.
item.content:encoded:
Spielberg's new thriller centers on a massive U.S. conspiracy to hide the fact that aliens have been visiting Earth for decades. If anything, though, the movie's pleasures feel more retro than timely.

(Image credit: Niko Tavernise)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: From 'hot priest' to Ripley, Andrew Scott is an advocate for his characters
item.description: In Pressure, Scott plays an army meteorologist advising military officials on the date for the D-Day invasion. He previously starred in Fleabag and Ripley. Originally broadcast April 8, 2024.
item.content:encoded: In Pressure, Scott plays an army meteorologist advising military officials on the date for the D-Day invasion. He previously starred in Fleabag and Ripley. Originally broadcast April 8, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Remembering French-Iranian artist and writer Marjane Satrapi
item.description: Satrapi's groundbreaking graphic novel Persepolis introduced readers to life in Iran during the Islamic revolution and the Iran/Iraq war. She died June 4, 2026. Originally broadcast June 2, 2003.
item.content:encoded: Satrapi's groundbreaking graphic novel Persepolis introduced readers to life in Iran during the Islamic revolution and the Iran/Iraq war. She died June 4, 2026. Originally broadcast June 2, 2003.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: 'Stop! That! Train!' is Loud! Dumb! and Gay!
item.description: It's camp. It's drag. A Stormaganza is coming and the Glamazonian Express is in trouble!
item.content:encoded:
It's camp. It's drag. A Stormaganza is coming and the Glamazonian Express is in trouble!

(Image credit: World of Wonder)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: David Hockney, one of the most influential artists of the 20th century, dies at 88
item.description: Hockney moved from London to Southern California in the 1960s and was an innovative painter, photographer, stage designer and printmaker.
item.content:encoded: Hockney moved from London to Southern California in the 1960s and was an innovative painter, photographer, stage designer and printmaker.
item.dc:creator: Susan Stamberg

item.title: Comic Josh Johnson explains why he shares his insecurities on stage
item.description: "One of the most connected attributes of the human condition ... is just being flawed," The Daily Show anchor says. "We really connect with people on their faults." His new comedy special is Symphony.
item.content:encoded: "One of the most connected attributes of the human condition ... is just being flawed," The Daily Show anchor says. "We really connect with people on their faults." His new comedy special is Symphony.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Trumpeter Adam O'Farrill mentors the next wave of jazz virtuosos on 'ELEPHANT'
item.description: O'Farrill is an introspective player whose solos insinuate themselves to listeners. His new album proves he's as adept at matching wits with his peers and elders as he is nurturing his protégés.
item.content:encoded: O'Farrill is an introspective player whose solos insinuate themselves to listeners. His new album proves he's as adept at matching wits with his peers and elders as he is nurturing his protégés.
item.dc:creator: Martin Johnson
